question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a crafted operator accountId string (e.g., '0.0.2.extra') that passes the split('\\.',3) length check but contains non-numeric segments, causing Long.valueOf() to throw NumberFormatException during init(), crashing the monitor at startup and preventing all transaction gossip?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a configuration value for hiero.mirror.monitor.operator.accountId that is an empty string, bypassing the @NotBlank constraint at the property binding level but causing a NullPointerException in init() when split() is called, halting the monitor and blocking all gossip?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user provide an operator accountId with a mismatched shard/realm that is not the DEFAULT_OPERATOR_ACCOUNT_ID ('0.0.2'), triggering the IllegalArgumentException branch in init() and preventing the monitor from starting, thus blocking all transaction gossip permanently?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user set the operator accountId to a value where accountId[0] and accountId[1] are valid longs matching shard/realm but accountId[2] is an extremely long string, causing operator.setAccountId() to produce an oversized account ID that is rejected by the Hedera SDK, preventing gossip of all transactions?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getMirrorNode()] Can an unprivileged external user leave mirrorNode null and set network to HederaNetwork.OTHER, causing getMirrorNode() to return a MirrorNodeProperties with an empty-string host, making all mirror node connections fail and preventing the address book retrieval needed for gossip node selection?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getMirrorNode()] Can an unprivileged external user configure mirrorNode with a malicious or unreachable host, causing getMirrorNode() to return a poisoned MirrorNodeProperties that routes address book requests to an attacker-controlled server, allowing the attacker to return an empty or invalid node list and prevent gossip?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user set the operator accountId to '0.0.' (empty third segment), causing the formatted string '%d.%d.%s' to produce '0.0.' which is an invalid Hedera account ID, making all subsequent transaction signing fail and preventing gossip?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user supply an operator accountId where Long.valueOf(accountId[0]) throws NumberFormatException due to a non-numeric shard value, crashing init() before any node validation occurs and preventing all transaction gossip from starting?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user set commonProperties shard/realm to values that never match any valid operator accountId, causing init() to always throw IllegalArgumentException for non-default accounts, effectively preventing the monitor from ever gossiping transactions on a custom network?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that init() uses Long.valueOf() (boxing) instead of long comparison, causing an autoboxing equality bug where shard/realm values above 127 are never equal via ==, so the condition always falls through to the exception branch, preventing gossip?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user set the operator accountId to a string with Unicode or special characters that passes @NotBlank but causes AccountId.fromString() in NodeSupplier.toClient() to throw an exception, preventing client creation and blocking all transaction gossip?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user set the operator privateKey to an invalid or malformed key string that passes @NotBlank but causes PrivateKey.fromString() to throw in NodeSupplier.toClient(), preventing all clients from being created and blocking all transaction gossip?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/MonitorProperties.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: init()] Can an unprivileged external user configure the nodes set to an empty LinkedHashSet and set nodeValidation.retrieveAddressBook=false and network=OTHER, causing NodeSupplier.refresh() to emit an error ('Nodes must not be empty'), making NodeSupplier.get() always throw and preventing all transaction gossip?
